topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm, the crisp bills a shocking contrast to the grime under my fingernails. It was tucked beneath a park bench, a forgotten treasure. My heart began to thump a frantic rhythm against my ribs. I carefully opened it, the sheer volume of cash overwhelming. A cold dread washed over me, tightening my chest until it was hard to draw a full breath. I looked around, half-expecting someone to materialize, their eyes sharp with accusation. I slipped it into my jacket pocket, the weight a physical burden, and started walking, my steps quick and uneven, desperate to put distance between myself and the discovery. Every glance in a shop window felt like a spotlight, burning into my neck.

[story 2]

I found the purse near the entrance of the subway station. Inside, nestled among old receipts, was a wad of money that made my stomach clench. It was an impossible amount. When I finally managed to secure it, the adrenaline that had fueled my search evaporated, leaving behind a hollow, sickening feeling. I sat on a cold stone wall, staring at the money as if it were a live creature. My hands trembled as I counted, the numbers blurring before my eyes. I wanted to shove it back, to erase the moment entirely, but the sheer reality of it held me captive. I spent the next hour pacing my small apartment, the silence amplifying the frantic drumming inside my head, wishing I could simply dissolve into the floorboards.

[story 3]

It was an unexpected find, a thick leather wallet lying abandoned on the sidewalk near the library. When I opened it, the sight of the bundled currency stopped my breath entirely. A wave of intense shame washed over me, so profound it made my skin crawl. I quickly tucked the wallet away, my movements jerky and unnatural. I kept replaying the moment in my mind—the casualness of the find, the sudden, staggering magnitude of the contents. I found myself avoiding eye contact with everyone, my shoulders hunched as if trying to physically shrink away from the world. Later that evening, I couldn't focus on anything; the money seemed to vibrate in my mind, a constant, unwelcome reminder of a boundary I had clearly crossed.
